concerns.text: FR‑025 (a priori power‑analysis guaranteeing N ≥ 300) is not represented by any implementation task, leaving the power‑analysis guarantee orphaned.
FR‑026 (Monte‑Carlo validation of each statistical test with ≤ 0.01 error) has no dedicated task, so the required validation step is missing.
FR‑032 (subgroup Fisher‑exact tests per domain/year) is absent from the task list; no task creates the required subgroup reports.
Principle VII (source‑provenance metadata retention) is not addressed by any task; URLs and domain identifiers are never recorded alongside extracted metrics.
Principle I (random‑seed pinning for reproducibility) is missing; no task ensures seeds are fixed in code or synthetic data generation.
Principle VI (any > 0.05 statistical discrepancy must be flagged *and* documented) lacks a dedicated task to capture and store discrepancy documentation in the audit notebook.
SC‑003 (Monte‑Carlo vs. library result difference ≤ 0.01) is not covered by any verification task.
SC‑005 (parsing‑error rate ≤ 5 % of total) is not validated; logging exists (FR‑007) but no aggregation or check task is defined.
SC‑025 (audit corpus size N ≥ 300 or power‑analysis minimum) is not enforced; the pipeline could run on a smaller set without raising an error.
SC‑028 (quickstart execution ≤ 30 minutes by a novice) lacks a verification task such as a timing benchmark or user‑test.
SC‑032 (subgroup prevalence & Fisher’s exact test for domains/years with ≥ 10 summaries) is not implemented; no task performs the required subgroup analysis.
Manifest .json content‑hash generation (Principle V) is only mentioned in T013 without any validation task to ensure hashes match the actual artifacts.
Bias‑adjusted prevalence (FR‑027) is implemented in T033, but no task verifies that the bias‑adjusted rate is actually written to the final CSV/JSON outputs as required by FR‑027 and SC‑027.
